reasoning: The major near-term hurdle for ED stock against the unadjusted baseline of 104.20 is the impending ex-dividend date on May 14, 2025, which will knock roughly $0.85 off the price. Over the next week (by May 18), expected price return drift is negligible compared to this drop. Using an estimated annualized volatility of 20%, the stock has a ~38% chance of overcoming this dividend hurdle to jump back past 104.20 within just 5 days. For June 10, the stock has slightly more time (~1 month) for its ~4.5% expected annual price drift to help it recover, bringing the probability closer to 46%. For August 9, only one dividend drop will have been incurred, while 3 months of drift will have accumulated, creating a near-even neutral outcome around 51%. By November 7, a second dividend drop (Q3) will have hit, but 6 months of expected drift almost perfectly offsets the $1.70 in accumulated dividend payouts, keeping the long-term unadjusted odds balanced very close to 52%. Standard deviation dominates the effect of drift and dividends over long horizons, driving the estimates toward 0.50 as uncertainty increases.
